summary: Referrals fell sharply starting in FY24 because DVS made mental health screenings voluntary rather than automatic for clients, which meant fewer people were screened and therefore fewer were referred out — the agency says this was a deliberate choice to avoid assuming every Veteran who contacts DVS needs mental health support. The partial rebound in FY26 followed a new intake system (using a more detailed suicide-risk screening tool) that DVS adopted mid-year.

sources.label: DVS, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report
